pr.title: lb: match fn_80026650 (100%), improve fn_800268B4 and lb_8000FD48
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: PR #2509 matched fn_80026650 in lbaudio_ax.c to 100% and made small score improvements to fn_800268B4 and lb_8000FD48. The changes used direct named externs for two lbaudio arrays, rewrote one lbaudio loop as a pointer-increment form, and factored repeated free-list pop code in lb_00F9.c into a static inline helper.

key_files.role: Matched fn_80026650 and improved fn_800268B4 by switching from lbl_80433710 struct-field accesses to direct named extern arrays and by using a pointer-increment loop form in fn_800268B4.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: src/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax.c added 15 deleted 13 hunks 3
human_pr_text.md: summary bullets for fn_80026650 and fn_800268B4
raw_diff: lbaudio_ax.c hunks around fn_80026650 and fn_800268B4
text_corpus.jsonl: decomp-dev bot report for fn_80026650 and fn_800268B4

key_files.role: Improved lb_8000FD48 by extracting duplicated cur_data free-list pop logic into static inline popDynamicsData.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: src/melee/lb/lb_00F9.c added 13 deleted 18 hunks 2
human_pr_text.md: summary bullet for lb_8000FD48
raw_diff: lb_00F9.c hunk adding popDynamicsData and replacing duplicated pop blocks
text_corpus.jsonl: decomp-dev bot report for lb_8000FD48

what_changed: In src/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax.c, fn_80026650 now initializes arr_38a4 and arr_3984 from lbl_804338A4 and lbl_80433984 instead of lbl_80433710.x194 and lbl_80433710.x274.

In src/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax.c, fn_800268B4 uses the same direct named extern arrays, changes the loop condition from i > 0 to i != 0, reads through dereferenced incrementing pointers, and increments arr_38a4, arr_3984, and arr_4e4 at the end of each loop iteration.

In src/melee/lb/lb_00F9.c, repeated cur_data pop logic inside lb_8000FD48 was moved into static inline popDynamicsData, which returns NULL for an empty list, advances cur_data, clears entry->next, and returns the popped entry.

The PR body reports fn_80026650 improved from 78.6% baseline to 100%, fn_800268B4 from 88.5% to 88.7%, and lb_8000FD48 from 93.7% to 94.2%; the automated bot report records fn_80026650 as a new 100.00% match and two smaller unmatched-item improvements.

smart_moves.title: Use narrower named externs instead of a broad struct anchor when the arrays are already named
smart_moves.text: The lbaudio changes replaced lbl_80433710.x194/x274 with direct extern arrays lbl_804338A4/lbl_80433984. In this PR, that direct access was tied to fn_80026650 reaching 100% and a small fn_800268B4 improvement.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: named extern swap described for fn_80026650 and fn_800268B4
raw_diff: lbaudio_ax.c arr_38a4/arr_3984 initializer replacements

smart_moves.title: Try pointer-increment loops for CTR-loop shaped code
smart_moves.text: fn_800268B4 moved from index-based accesses to dereferencing and incrementing array pointers. The PR body states this produced a prologue using bdnz/mtctr CTR-loop matching the expected layout.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: pointer-increment loop form and bdnz/mtctr note
raw_diff: lbaudio_ax.c fn_800268B4 pointer dereference and pointer increments

smart_moves.title: Factor duplicated list-pop code into a local static inline when nearby style supports it
smart_moves.text: lb_8000FD48 had two duplicated cur_data pop blocks replaced with popDynamicsData. The PR body says the helper mirrors an existing inlineA0 pattern and improved the function score.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: popDynamicsData described as mirroring inlineA0
raw_diff: lb_00F9.c popDynamicsData addition and call sites

decomp_lessons.lesson: For matching work, a direct named extern can be preferable to accessing the same data through a larger struct anchor when the PR evidence shows the narrower symbol improves the function match.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: direct lbl_804338A4/lbl_80433984 extern use credited for fn_80026650 match and fn_800268B4 improvement
raw_diff: lbl_80433710.x194/x274 replaced by lbl_804338A4/lbl_80433984

decomp_lessons.lesson: Pointer-increment loop forms can be worth testing when an indexed loop is close but misses CTR-loop/prologue shape.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: fn_800268B4 pointer-increment loop form improved 88.5% to 88.7% and matched bdnz/mtctr prologue layout
raw_diff: fn_800268B4 loop changed to dereference and post-increment arr_38a4, arr_3984, arr_4e4

decomp_lessons.lesson: A static inline helper can improve repeated free-list manipulation when it captures the repeated operation and matches an existing local inline pattern.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: popDynamicsData static inline mirrors existing inlineA0 pattern and improves lb_8000FD48
raw_diff: duplicated cur_data pop logic replaced by popDynamicsData calls

rejection_notes: Do not generalize from this PR that all lbl_80433710 struct-anchor uses should be replaced; evidence is limited to fn_80026650 and fn_800268B4 in lbaudio_ax.c.
Do not promote pointer-increment loops as always preferable; this PR only supports trying that form when it improves the observed CTR-loop shape for this function.
Do not treat the reported build/checkdiff verification as an independently loaded log; only the PR body and bot score report are available.
